behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is primarily backoff math/state progression logic, which can be exercised within one retry-policy object/function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess is required to reproduce wrong doubling or cap behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on timing formula ('doubles... up to configured maximum'), which is local logic.
behaviors.test_description: Test a retry/backoff policy function with consecutive failure counts and max cap config; assert wait sequence (e.g., 100, 200, 400, 800, 800).

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module handoff (HTTP response mapping -> retry classifier -> backoff computation). Unit tests of each piece can miss mismatched wiring (e.g., wrong status/header interpreted). REAL-INFRA: real network not required; in-process stubbed responses are sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior text is about identification/computation logic, not real HTTP conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire client + response parser + retry policy with a stub transport returning 429/ratelimit payload; assert retry path is taken and computed delay matches policy/header precedence.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires orchestration across timer/backoff, request replay, and response unwrapping; single-function tests can miss request mutation/loss across retries. REAL-INFRA: fake clock + stub transport can reproduce this exactly; no real OS/network needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior does not require real network timing, only resend orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Using fake timers and stub transport (first 429 then 200), call `call()` once, advance clock, verify second request payload equals original and resolved value is successful response body without retry details leaking.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: typed error emerges from interaction of retry counter exhaustion + error translation layer; unit-only checks can miss incorrect propagation from lower transport error to public API error type. REAL-INFRA: deterministic stub failures suffice; no real HTTP needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior text is about terminal mapping after retry count, not infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Configure low max retries, stub transport to always return rate-limit failure, execute `call()`, assert rejection is instance of `QuotaExhaustedError` with expected metadata and not generic Error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module control-flow safety (retry loop + quota bookkeeping + stop condition). Unit tests of loop helper alone may miss state-handoff bugs that cause non-termination. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS is necessary; fake timers + stub transport catch infinite-loop risk. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior specifies internal retry chain termination, reproducible fully in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Run client with fake timers and always-rate-limited stub transport; advance time through all scheduled retries; assert promise settles within bounded virtual time and attempts count equals configured limit.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure is at shared-state boundary across concurrent callers and client instances/modules, not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: in-process concurrency (Promises) is enough; no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior text explicitly scopes to 'same process' shared bucket, which is testable via local module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate callers sharing library state, fire concurrent `call()` invocations against stub transport, assert global quota counters/permit decisions reflect one shared bucket rather than per-caller isolated buckets.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bugs appear in interaction between queue, scheduler, and retry/quota gate; unit-testing queue alone can miss orchestration reorderings. REAL-INFRA: deterministic in-process scheduler/fake timers can reproduce FIFO violations; real network not required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior concerns submission-order dispatch policy, not external infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Queue multiple pending requests with controlled quota release and fake timers; record transport send order and assert it matches submission order exactly under contention.
